description: DB integration: first billing-settings ensure for a client creates exactly one system-managed default contract + one assignment (idempotent on repeated call).
Concurrency integration: parallel ensure calls for same tenant+client produce no duplicate default contracts.
Unit+integration: time-entry save resolves default line by effective work date (covers backdated, current, future dates).
Unit+integration: usage create/update resolves default line by effective usage date and remains consistent with UI eligibility endpoint.
Billing-engine integration: unresolved time row with exactly one eligible line is persisted with contract_line_id and removed from unresolved output.
Billing-engine integration: unresolved usage row with exactly one eligible line is persisted with contract_line_id and removed from unresolved output.
Due-work integration: ambiguous or no-match records remain unresolved and visible; deterministic records do not surface as non-contract candidates.
Invoice-generation integration: selected recurring rows generate correctly with default-contract-aware keys and no primary-path dependence on legacy non-contract IDs.
UI test: automatic invoicing grouped rows show business labels for default-contract and unmatched records; metadata-missing rows are hard-blocked.
UI test: contract list/detail show system-managed default badge/helper copy and hide/disable disallowed destructive/ownership actions.
UI test: time-entry and usage banners/messages use default-contract terminology and do not imply a persisted default when assignment is unresolved.
Lifecycle integration: client delete through package action removes billing settings/cycles and default-contract assignment artifacts without dangling references.
Lifecycle integration: client delete through API/controller path executes equivalent cleanup behavior (no orphaned default-contract links).
Integration: null-setting override/deletion paths do not recreate default contract unexpectedly, while valid active billing-configuration paths still ensure it.
Migration test: schema migration for markers/pointers/uniqueness is idempotent and safe on already-migrated databases.
Regression integration: service-period and contract-cadence recurring invoicing scenarios remain correct after default-contract cutover.
Observability test: ensure/reconcile/route paths emit expected structured logs/markers with no PII leakage.
Integration-import regression: clients created via integration pathways still receive default contract on first qualifying billing-configuration interaction.
